title: prunus subcoriacea
intro: Prunus subcoriacea, also known as the Australian plum or Kakadu plum, is a plant native to the tropical and subtropical regions of northern Australia. It has been used for centuries in traditional Aboriginal medicine due to its rich source of vitamin C and various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The fruit and leaves of the plant have been used to treat a range of health issues, including colds, fever, and digestive problems, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the Kakadu plum is often used as a raw ingredient in supplements, jams, and sauces, as well as in skincare products to promote skin health and reduce the appearance of fine lines and wrinkles.
